_README: Golden fixture for the refresh-cursor capture-cursor primitive. Bytes = lines.map(l=>l+String.fromCharCode(10)).join("") + (trailing||""). expected = {event_id, offset} or null.

vectors.name: multi_line
vectors.doc: three complete lines -> cursor at the 3rd

vectors.name: partial_trailing_line
vectors.doc: complete lines + a torn trailing write (no newline) -> cursor at last COMPLETE line, torn bytes ignored

vectors.name: growth_after
vectors.doc: growth_before + one appended line -> cursor advances

vectors.name: multibyte
vectors.doc: a 4-byte astral char (rocket) in a value -> offset counts BYTES not chars

vectors.name: corrupt_last_line_fallback
vectors.doc: last newline-terminated line is invalid JSON -> fall back to previous complete line boundary

vectors.name: no_complete_line
vectors.doc: only a torn partial, no newline yet -> null (nothing captured through)

vectors.name: trailing_metadata_single
vectors.doc: identity record then one uuid-less metadata line -> cursor anchors to the identity line (cursor-neutral rule)

vectors.name: trailing_metadata_multi
vectors.doc: two identity records then permission-mode + bridge-session tail -> cursor anchors to last identity line; metadata churn moves nothing

vectors.name: metadata_then_torn
vectors.doc: identity record, metadata line, then a torn trailing segment -> identity boundary (both fallback rules compose)

vectors.name: metadata_only
vectors.doc: only uuid-less metadata lines -> null (not-ready), never a null event_id cursor

vectors.name: corrupt_between_identity_and_metadata
vectors.doc: identity record, corrupt line, metadata line -> torn-line and cursor-neutral fallbacks compose to the identity boundary

vectors.name: message_id_fallback_with_metadata
vectors.doc: message.id identity (no uuid) then metadata tail -> message id anchors the cursor
